device_name: EFAI Chestsuite XR Malpositioned ETT Assessment System (ETT-XR-100)
intended_use: EFAI CHESTSUITE XR MALPOSITIONED ETT ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (EFAI ETTXR) is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest X-ray (CXR) images in a
intended_use_full: EFAI CHESTSUITE XR MALPOSITIONED ETT ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (EFAI ETTXR) is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of chest X-ray (CXR) images in adults. The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communicating suspected positive cases of vertically malpositioned endotracheal tube (ETT) in relation to the carina. Findings are flagged when the ETT distal tip is assessed as being more than 7 cm above the carina, less than 3 cm above the carina, or when it is below the carina (i.e in the right or left mainstem bronchus).
target_condition: Vertically malpositioned endotracheal tube (ETT) relative to the carina
ground_truth: Majority agreement among three U.S. board-certified radiologists; reference standard classified 259 positive cases (malpositioned ETT) and 681 negative cases (316 correctly positioned ETT and 365 with no ETT)
other_metrics: Secondary endpoint: mean system processing time per study is 2.49 minutes (95% CI=2.43-2.56 minutes)
